nodes.title: 聚类方法与EEND的各自局限

sections.body: Clustering-based diarization methods partition frames into clusters; each frame is assigned to at most one speaker, so they fundamentally cannot handle overlapping speech. End-to-end diarization (EEND) methods treat diarization as multi-label classification and can handle overlaps, but they perform poorly when the number of speakers is large—due to overtraining on mixtures with few speakers and the computational cost of permutation invariant training. DIHARD II exemplifies this: clustering-based methods remain strong baselines on datasets with large numbers of speakers [4].

nodes.title: 迭代双人EEND后处理方法概述

sections.body: We propose to combine clustering-based and end-to-end methods: first obtain initial diarization results via x-vector clustering, then iteratively select two speakers, pick frames containing only those two speakers, and refine their results using a two-speaker EEND model $f_{\text{EEND}}$. We assume at most $\widetilde{K} < K$ speakers speak simultaneously; here $\widetilde{K} = 2$. The method compensates for each approach's weakness—clustering handles many speakers, EEND handles overlaps.

Given initial diarization results $\{ \widehat{T}_k \subseteq [T] \}_{k=1}^{K}$, iteratively: (i) determine processing order of speaker pairs, (ii) for each pair $\{i, j\}$, select frames $P_{i,j}$ containing only speakers $i$ and $j$, (iii) apply $f_{\text{EEND}}$ to obtain two-speaker posteriors, (iv) solve speaker permutation, (v) update diarization results for $i, j$. When $K \ge 3$, only overlapped frames are updated via Equation (8).
To apply two-speaker EEND refinement on a pair $\{i, j\}$, frames containing speakers other than $i$ and $j$ are excluded. The selected frame set is defined as:

$$P_{i,j} = [T] \setminus \bigcup_{k \in [K] \setminus \{i,j\}} \widehat{T}_k \qquad (1)$$
where $[T] = \{1, \dots, T\}$ is the set of all frame indices and $\widehat{T}_k$ is the current estimated active frame set for speaker $k$. This selection is reapplied after each refinement step because $\{\widehat{T}_k\}$ gets updated.

nodes.title: 处理顺序确定：按帧数降序

sections.body: The processing order of speaker pairs influences final accuracy because estimated diarization results contain errors, causing impurities (frames of excluded speakers) in $P_{i,j}$. This is resolved by processing pairs in decreasing order of $|P_{i,j}|$: pairs with more selected frames are refined first, reducing cumulative error propagation.

nodes.title: 迭代更新与说话人置换求解

sections.body: For each speaker pair $\{i, j\}$ in descending order of $|P_{i,j}|$, frames $P_{i,j}$ are reselected via Eq. (1), then the EEND model outputs two-speaker posteriors. A permutation is solved to match the EEND output speakers (a,b) to the target speakers (i,j). Results are updated—fully when $K = 2$, or only on overlapped frames when $K \ge 3$.

The EEND model $f_{\text{EEND}}$ outputs posteriors $q_t^{(a)}, q_t^{(b)}$ for $t \in P_{i,j}$ via Eq. (2). Thresholding at 0.5 gives active frame sets $Q^{(a)}, Q^{(b)}$ via Eq. (3). Speaker permutation is solved by:
$$(\widetilde{T}_i, \widetilde{T}_j) = \arg\max_{(u,v) \in \{(a,b),(b,a)\}} \big[ s(Q^{(u)}, \widehat{T}_i) + s(Q^{(v)}, \widehat{T}_j) \big] \qquad (4)$$
where $s(U, V) = |U \cap V| + |([T] \setminus U) \cap ([T] \setminus V)|$ combines speech and non-speech similarity (Eq. 5). A condition (Eq. 6) with $\rho = 0.5$ checks that new results sufficiently overlap with previous ones before accepting. When $K = 2$, full update via Eq. (7); when $K \ge 3$, only overlapped frames are updated:
$$\widehat{T}_i \leftarrow \widehat{T}_i \setminus (\widetilde{T}_i \cap \widetilde{T}_j), \quad \widehat{T}_j \leftarrow \widehat{T}_j \setminus (\widetilde{T}_j \cap \widetilde{T}_j) \qquad (8)$$

nodes.title: 基于帧选择的模型适配训练

sections.body: Instead of using only two-speaker subsets for model adaptation (as in [6, 8, 9]), we adopt the frame-selection technique of Eq. (1) for training: if an input chunk contains more than two speakers, we choose two dominant speakers and eliminate frames where other speakers are active. The model is trained only on selected frames to output speech activities of the two speakers. This enables fine-tuning two-speaker EEND models on any multi-speaker dataset without mixture-wise selection.

nodes.title: SA-EEND-EDA：自注意力EEND+编解码吸引子

sections.body: SA-EEND-EDA [8] is a self-attentive end-to-end diarization model with an encoder-decoder attractor calculation module. It consists of a four-layer Transformer encoder extracting frame embeddings, and an EDA module using LSTM to calculate attractors from the embeddings (with shuffled input order for improved performance). The model can output a flexible number of speakers' activities and is order-independent, making it suitable for processing discontinuous selected frames.

nodes.title: SA-EEND：自注意力端到端说话人日志

sections.body: SA-EEND [6] is a self-attentive end-to-end neural diarization model that reformulates speaker diarization as multi-label classification using Transformer encoders. It predicts per-frame, per-speaker activity probabilities with permutation-invariant training.

nodes.title: 帧选择训练有效性验证

sections.body: Using the full CALLHOME dataset with frame selection for training (instead of CALLHOME-2spk subset) improved two-speaker DER on CALLHOME-2spk test: SA-EEND from 9.54% to 9.00%; SA-EEND-EDA from 8.07% to 7.84% (Table 2). This validates the training strategy of Section 3.3—frame selection enables effective use of multi-speaker mixtures for adapting two-speaker models.

On CALLHOME (telephone conversations, 2–6 speakers): x-vector AHC achieved 19.43% DER; with VB-HMM resegmentation 17.61% DER. Applying the proposed post-processing reduced these to 16.79% and 14.06% DER respectively. The combined method (AHC+VB+Proposed, 14.06%) outperformed SA-EEND-EDA alone (15.29% DER). Notably, clustering-based methods are better when speaker count > 4, and the proposed method performs well on both small and large numbers of speakers (Table 3).

On AMI eval set (meeting recordings, headset mix): the proposed method reduced DER by 3.07% (from 33.75% to 30.64%), 3.14% (from 32.80% to 29.66%), and 0.18% (from 28.15% to 27.97%) absolute over three baselines: x-vector AHC, AHC+VB, and AHC+VB+overlap detection [29], respectively (Table 4). JER also improved consistently. The method further improves results even when dedicated overlap detection [29] is already applied.

On DIHARD II eval set (10 diverse domains, 1–9 speakers): the proposed method reduced the official baseline DER from 40.86% to 37.90% (JER from 66.60% to 63.79%). It also improved the BUT system (winning system of DIHARD II Challenge): without overlap assignment, DER 27.26% → 26.91%; with overlap assignment, DER 27.11% → 26.88% (Table 5). These improvements, though modest (~0.35%), exceed the gain from the heuristic overlap assignment in [12] (0.15% DER).

nodes.title: 聚类方法在多人场景下仍占优

sections.body: On CALLHOME, clustering-based methods (x-vector AHC) achieve better DER than SA-EEND-EDA when the number of speakers exceeds 4 (e.g., 5 speakers: 31.40% vs 33.16%; 6 speakers: 34.27% vs 40.29%). This confirms that clustering-based methods, which do not use overlapping speech mixtures for training, do not suffer from overtraining on mixtures of few speakers and remain strong for large speaker counts.

nodes.title: 结论：后处理方法普遍有效

sections.body: The proposed iterative two-speaker EEND post-processing method consistently improves various clustering-based diarization results across CALLHOME, AMI, and DIHARD II datasets. It effectively handles overlapping speech regardless of the number of speakers by compensating for both the clustering methods' inability to handle overlaps and the end-to-end methods' difficulty with large speaker counts. The frame-selection training strategy further enables adaptation of two-speaker models on any multi-speaker dataset.
